MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-15-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-14-27 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court'S Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF VIA SENTI, A CALIFORNIA 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT , DBA HEIRS TO OUR OCEAN Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court'S Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF VIA SENTI, A CALIFORNIA 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT , DBA HEIRS TO OUR OCEAN's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court'S Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions;

Plaintiff Via Senti's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court's Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant, through counsel, violated the court's September 20, 2024, and their misconduct is not excused. Defendant's actions in connection with the discovery requests, order and responses at issue were not substantially justified. Defendant, shall pay $4,000.00 to Plaintiff in sanctions within 10 days of notice of this order, proof of payment to be filed with the court in the register of actions within 3 courts days of payment. As in the September 20, 2024 order, the court imposes sanctions on Defendant. If Defense counsel believes themselves responsible, the court trusts Defendant and counsel can work that out. The request for terminating sanctions is denied. The record does not support imposition if the most drastic sanctions available at this time. To the extent Plaintiff seeks additional relief, the motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Nyoki Sacramento, ESQ. of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran, LLP (Tel: 415-673-5600) for Plaintiff (Via Zoomvideoconference).
Atty. Roger Jackson, ESQ. (Tel: 707-318-2994) for Defendants.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF VIA SENTI, A CALIFORNIA 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT , DBA HEIRS TO OUR OCEAN Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court's Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions.

The court holds a discussion regarding the lack of an opposition. Counsel Roger Jackson states an opposition was filed by the defendant. Counsel Nyoki Sacramento states that the defendant has complied with the prior discovery order, and the plaintiff is no longer seeking terminating sanctions, only monetary sanctions. Upon conclusion, the court vacates its tentative ruling, and continues the matter to December 20, 2024 at 9:00 am in Department 525, to consider the opposition and the reply.
